summary: High‑energy set with scorching I’m A Man and vibrant Hornsby guest
blurb: The concert rides a wave of high‑octane energy, highlighted by an explosive I’m A Man and a sparkling C.C. Rider featuring Bruce Hornsby's piano. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Phil locks in with a funky intro, and the second set maintains momentum through Jack Straw into China Cat > I Know You Rider.
review: On December 10, 1989 at the Great Western LA Forum the Dead delivered a blistering performance that still feels fresh. From the opening of “C.C. Rider” straight into an incendiary “I’m A Man,” Jerry Garcia’s guitar is ripping and full‑throttle while Phil Lesh launches the song with a “sick” bass intro that set the tone for a night of relentless groove. Guest pianist Bruce Hornsby adds frantic piano and accordion sparkle, especially on the extended “China Cat Sunflower” jam, turning it into a bright, high‑energy centerpiece. The second set flows smoothly through Jack Straw, the classic China > Rider > I Know You Rider sequence, and a solid run of “Stella Blue,” with Brent Mydland’s B‑3 answering beautifully to Hornsby’s keys. Though “Stella Blue” shows a brief tonal hiccup for Jerry, the overall momentum never wanes, making this show one of the standout late‑‘80s outings. The soundboard capture (gd89-12-10.sbd.ladner.5451.sbeok.shnf) presents the band in crisp detail, preserving every nuance of that electrifying night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the I’m A Man jam, Hornsby’s guest work and overall high‑energy flow. No negative comments appear, indicating strong consensus of excellence across the show.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with clear, well‑balanced audio that showcases the high‑energy performance and Hornsby’s guest contributions.

key_highlights: Scorching “I’m A Man” jam with Jerry’s ripping guitar and Phil’s funky bass intro.
Bruce Hornsby’s frantic piano/accordion adds sparkle to C.C. Rider and the extended China Cat Sunflower jam.
Seamless set flow, especially the C.C. Rider → I’m A Man transition and the second‑set momentum through Jack Straw into China Cat > I Know You Rider.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is on fire, delivering energetic and ripping guitar work throughout, especially on I’m A Man; a brief odd tone at the start of Stella Blue is noted but doesn’t detract from his overall stellar performance.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a “sick” bass intro to I’m A Man and stays deeply into the song, channeling a Duck Dunn‑style groove that locks the band tight.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm work helps rescue C.C. Rider and adds solid support; his presence is noted as crucial in keeping the jam flowing.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland shines on B‑3, answers beautifully to Hornsby, and steps in to keep jams bright, especially during the China Cat and Rider sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s percussion contributes solidly throughout, maintaining the high‑energy drive of the set.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming is steady and supportive, providing a reliable backbone for the band’s explosive moments.
